title: Apple Inc. (AAPL) Q1 2024 Earnings Call Transcript
content:
Operator:
Good day, and welcome to the Apple Q1 2024 Earnings Conference Call. Today's call is being recorded. At this time, I would like to turn the call over to Suhasini Chandramouli, Director of Investor Relations. Please go ahead.

Suhasini Chandramouli - Director of Investor Relations:
Thank you. Good afternoon, and thank you for joining us. Speaking first today is Apple CEO Tim Cook, and he'll be followed by CFO Luca Maestri. After that, we'll open the call to questions from analysts.

Tim Cook - CEO:
Thank you, Suhasini. Good afternoon, everyone. We are pleased to report record revenue of $119.6 billion for the December quarter. This represents strong growth driven by our services business and iPhone sales. We continue to see tremendous customer satisfaction and loyalty across our product lineup. Our installed base of active devices has reached an all-time high, which fuels our growing services ecosystem.

Luca Maestri - CFO:
Thank you, Tim. Revenue for the quarter was $119.6 billion, up 2% year over year. Gross margin was 45.9%, near the high end of our guidance range. Operating expenses were well managed, and we generated strong operating cash flow of $39.9 billion. We returned over $27 billion to shareholders during the quarter through dividends and share repurchases.

Operator:
We will now begin the question-and-answer session. Our first question comes from the line of Erik Woodring with Morgan Stanley.

Erik Woodring - Morgan Stanley:
Thanks for taking my question. Tim, can you talk about the trajectory of iPhone demand and how you see the upgrade cycle playing out with the new AI features?

Tim Cook - CEO:
Sure, Erik. We're seeing broad-based strength in iPhone demand across all geographies. The AI capabilities we've introduced are resonating very strongly with customers. We believe this positions us well for a multi-year upgrade cycle as more users seek to access these features on compatible hardware.
